channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A rocket from Yemen strikes Tel Aviv, injuring 16, and Palestinians mourn a dozen children in Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — A rocket fired from Yemen hit an area of Tel Aviv overnight, leaving 16 people injured by shattered glass, the Israeli military said Saturday, days after Israeli airstrikes hit Houthi rebels who have been launching missiles in solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: What mayor Eric Adams told Luigi Mangione when they met in NYC
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he “wanted to send a strong message” when he met Luigi Mangione on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, NewsNation

item.title: Blake Lively accuses 'It Ends With Us' director Justin Baldoni of harassment and smear campaign
item.description: Blake Lively has accused her “It Ends With Us” director and co-star Justin Baldoni of sexual harassment on the set of the movie and a subsequent effort to “destroy” her reputation in a legal complaint.
item.dc:creator: MALLIKA SEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Blake Lively accuses 'It Ends With Us' director Justin Baldoni of harassment, smear campaign
item.description: In the complaint, Lively accuses Baldoni and the studio of embarking on a “multi-tiered plan” to damage her reputation following a meeting in which she and her husband Ryan Reynolds addressed “repeated sexual harassment and other disturbing behavior” by Baldoni and a producer on the movie.
item.dc:creator: Mallika Sen, KRON4

item.title: Ex-OpenAI engineer who raised legal concerns about the technology he helped build has died
item.description: Suchir Balaji, a former OpenAI engineer and whistleblower who helped train the artificial intelligence systems behind ChatGPT and later said he believed those practices violated copyright law, has died, according to his parents and San Francisco officials.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Here's a look at the $100 billion in disaster relief in the government spending bill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congress is allocating more than $100 billion in emergency aid designed to address extensive damage caused by disasters after this week's scramble to find consensus on a government spending bill.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Orphaned elk raised by Oregon cows accepted by local elk herd
item.description: An orphaned elk calf that turned up at a Pacific City ranch in March was recently accepted by a herd of elk after spending nearly nine months living with a herd of Angus beef cows.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Hanukkah and how it's celebrated around the world
item.description: Hanukkah — also spelled Chanukah or other transliterations from Hebrew — is Judaism’s “festival of lights.” On eight consecutive nightfalls, Jews gather with family and friends to light one additional candle in the menorah — a multibranched candelabra.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here are the 34 Republicans that voted against the funding bill to avoid shutdown
item.description: More than 30 House Republicans voted against the government funding bill to avert a shutdown on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: US flu season is underway, as cases surge in some areas and vaccinations lag
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. flu season is underway, with cases surging across much of the country, health officials said Friday. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention noted sharp increases in several measures, including lab tests and emergency room visits.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 of the most frustrating health insurer tactics and why they exist
item.description: Even for those with health insurance, coverage does not ensure access to care, much less high-quality and affordable care.
item.dc:creator: Monica S. Aswani Assistant Professor of Health Services Administration, University of Alabama at Birmingham, ABC 4

item.title: Starbucks workers plan strikes that could hit hundreds of stores by Christmas Eve
item.description: Workers at Starbucks stores plan to go on a five-day strike starting Friday to protest lack of progress in contract negotiations with the company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Traveling for the holidays? These are the busiest times to fly or drive
item.description: Drivers and airline passengers without reindeer and sleighs better make a dash for it: It's beginning to look like another record for holiday travel in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Koenig, KRON4

item.title: Hochul signs law to stop unauthorized restaurant reservations
item.description: Hochul signed a bill on requiring digital platforms to contract directly with restaurants in order to sell reservations there.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: US civil rights probe faults Philadelphia school district over handling of antisemitism reports
item.description: The Philadelphia school district failed to show it protected Jewish students from harassment despite “repeated, extensive notice” that students, teachers and administrators were engaging in antisemitic behavior, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL RUBINKAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How drone activity could affect US holiday travel plans
item.description: Recent drone sightings around the country have raised concerns over whether drone activity could affect holiday travel
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, ABC 4

item.title: Government shutdown is averted just after deadline as Congress rejects Trump's debt limit demands
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Farnoush Amiri And Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ohio lawmakers approve charging up to $750 for police and jail videos
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — People seeking copies of police and jail videos in Ohio may have to pay up to $75 for each hour if Gov. Mike DeWine signs a measure approved by the state legislature this week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More student loans canceled in final round of forgiveness before Biden leaves office
item.description: With the latest round of relief, Biden has now canceled an unprecedented $180 billion in federal student loans through existing programs, covering 4.9 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: A day before a potential government shutdown, the House resoundingly rejected President-elect Donald Trump's new plan to fund operations and suspend the debt ceiling, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Young nuns: Two diverse paths to a rare life of faith
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Less than 1% of Catholic nuns in the United States today are 30 or younger. Seyram Adzokpa and Zoey Stapleton are two of the young women who have made the rare decision to join a religious community and begin the long process to become nuns. Here are their stories.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan school shooter loses bid to withdraw guilty plea in 4 deaths
item.description: PONTIAC, Mich. (AP) — A judge on Thursday refused to allow a Michigan school shooter to withdraw his guilty plea to two dozen charges, including terrorism and first-degree murder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kansas restaurant worker caught chopping meat on ground, owner explains why
item.description: The video shows an employee cutting up or hammering meat on the sidewalk or drive-thru pavement area right outside Tryyaki.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, ABC 4

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Grim new term coined to describe Oct. 7 phenomenon
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO will return to New York to face murder charges
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO will return to New York to face murder charges after agreeing to be extradited Thursday during a court appearance in Pennsylvania where he was arrested last week after five days on the run.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US effort to curb China's and Russia's access to advanced computer chips 'inadequate,' report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Commerce Department's efforts to curb China's and Russia's access to American-made advanced computer chips have been “inadequate” and will need more funding to stymie their ability to manufacture advanced weapons, according to a report published Wednesday by the Senate's Perman
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NASA astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, already stuck in space, are hit with new delay
item.description: Astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, the crew of the first manned Boeing Starliner launch in early June, were initially meant to spend only about eight days in space.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4
